evals.prompt: Run a full component audit for our CJA data view. I want to see the health of both our segments and calculated metrics in one report. Data view: Adobe Store - Web, imsOrgId: lumaac0@AdobeOrg
evals.expected_output: The skill should: (1) call findDataViews to locate the target data view and call setDefaultSessionDataViewId to set it as the session default, (2) collect segment inventory via findSegments(limit: 1000, expansions: 'ownerFullName,modified,isDeleted,definition,dataId,dataName,tags,usageSummary,usageSummaryWithRelevancyScore,createdDate,recentRecordedAccess') and calculated metric inventory via findCalculatedMetrics(limit: 1000, expansions: 'ownerFullName,modified,isDeleted,definition,dataId,dataName,tags,usageSummary,usageSummaryWithRelevancyScore,createdDate,recentRecordedAccess'), (3) call listComponentUsage for both componentType 'segment' and componentType 'calculatedMetric' to obtain usage rankings, (4) classify all components into Active/Stale/Unused tiers based on last access dates and usage counts, (5) run 4-tier duplicate detection for segments (exact definition match, semantic equivalence, name similarity, container-structure match) and formula-based duplicate detection for calculated metrics, plus name-similarity scanning across both types, (6) compute a per-type health score (0-100) using the formula: activeCount/totalCount*50 minus 5 per duplicate group minus 10 if more than 50% unused plus 10 if fewer than 5% name-similar pairs, then compute an overall weighted health score with segments and calculated metrics each weighted 2x, (7) save all inventory data to JSON files and run cja_component_audit.py to generate a combined HTML report. The HTML report must contain: an Executive Summary section with combined KPIs (total components, overall health score, active/stale/unused counts across both types, total duplicate groups found), a Segments section with tier breakdown and duplicate groups, a Calculated Metrics section with tier breakdown and formula-duplicate groups, a Duplicates and Near-Duplicates section covering both types with merge/delete recommendations, and a unified Recommendations section with action items tagged by component type using icons (delete, merge, rename, archive review, tag).

evals.prompt: We have a governance committee meeting before Q2 planning and need a thorough audit of all CJA segments and calculated metrics to identify what should be cleaned up. We need unused components flagged for deletion, duplicate groups consolidated, a combined health score across both types, and every recommendation must include the owner's full name so the committee knows who to contact. Data view: B2B Demand Gen - Global, imsOrgId: adobeenterprise@AdobeOrg
evals.expected_output: The skill should: (1) call findDataViews to identify the 'B2B Demand Gen - Global' data view and call setDefaultSessionDataViewId to set it as the session default, (2) retrieve the full segment inventory via findSegments(limit: 1000, expansions: 'ownerFullName,modified,isDeleted,definition,dataId,dataName,tags,usageSummary,usageSummaryWithRelevancyScore,createdDate,recentRecordedAccess') and the full calculated metric inventory via findCalculatedMetrics(limit: 1000, expansions: 'ownerFullName,modified,isDeleted,definition,dataId,dataName,tags,usageSummary,usageSummaryWithRelevancyScore,createdDate,recentRecordedAccess'), (3) call listComponentUsage for both 'segment' and 'calculatedMetric' to rank components by usage frequency, (4) classify components into Active/Stale/Unused tiers; components with zero usage and last access more than 90 days ago should be flagged as primary deletion candidates, (5) perform duplicate detection for segments using the 4-tier approach (exact definition, semantic equivalence, name similarity, container-structure) and for calculated metrics using formula comparison and name similarity; each duplicate group must list the ownerFullName of every member, (6) conduct ownership analysis: count unused components per owner, identify single-owner risk (components whose only knowledgeable owner has left or has >20 unused components), and surface knowledge transfer risks, (7) compute per-type and overall weighted health scores (segments and calculated metrics each weighted 2x) and include score rationale in the report, (8) save inventory JSON files and run cja_component_audit.py to produce a governance-ready combined HTML report. The report must include: an Executive Summary with combined KPIs and the overall weighted health score, a Segments section and a Calculated Metrics section each with tier breakdowns, a Duplicates section listing each duplicate group with owner names and a recommended primary component to retain, an Ownership Distribution section showing per-owner unused counts and knowledge transfer risk flags, and a Recommendations section where every action item (delete, merge, rename, archive review, tag) includes the ownerFullName of the responsible stakeholder so the governance committee can assign follow-up actions directly.

evals.prompt: Run an extended CJA component audit that covers not just segments and calculated metrics, but also dimensions and metrics. I want to see cross-type dependencies — which segments reference which dimensions, which calculated metrics reference which metrics — and flag any orphaned references to deleted or deprecated components. Also call out any dimensions or metrics owned by a single person. Data view: Retail Commerce - Americas, imsOrgId: retailcorp@AdobeOrg
evals.expected_output: The skill should: (1) confirm with the user (or proceed by default) that the extended audit will include all four component types: segments, calculated metrics, dimensions, and metrics, (2) call findDataViews to locate 'Retail Commerce - Americas' and call setDefaultSessionDataViewId to set it as the session default, (3) retrieve all four inventories: findSegments(limit: 1000, expansions: 'ownerFullName,modified,isDeleted,definition,dataId,dataName,tags,usageSummary,usageSummaryWithRelevancyScore,createdDate,recentRecordedAccess'), findCalculatedMetrics(limit: 1000, expansions: 'ownerFullName,modified,isDeleted,definition,dataId,dataName,tags,usageSummary,usageSummaryWithRelevancyScore,createdDate,recentRecordedAccess'), findDimensions(limit: 1000), and findMetrics(limit: 1000), (4) call listComponentUsage for all four types: 'segment', 'calculatedMetric', 'dimension', and 'metric', (5) classify components across all four types into Active/Stale/Unused tiers, (6) for each segment, parse its definition to extract every dimension ID it references; for each calculated metric, parse its formula to extract every metric ID it references; cross-reference these against the dimension and metric inventories to build a cross-type dependency map, (7) identify orphaned references: segment definitions that reference dimension IDs marked as isDeleted or not present in the current findDimensions result, and calculated metric formulas that reference metric IDs marked as isDeleted or not present in the current findMetrics result; surface these as high-priority remediation items, (8) for the top segments and calculated metrics by usage, call listSimilarTo(componentId, componentType) and listFrequentlyUsedWith(componentId, componentType) to discover API-driven similarity and co-usage patterns that inform merge and consolidation candidates, (9) conduct ownership analysis across all four types: flag any dimension or metric whose ownerFullName appears for fewer than two active users (single-owner risk), count per-owner unused components, and highlight knowledge transfer risks, (10) compute per-type health scores (segments and calc metrics weighted 2x, dimensions and metrics weighted 1x each) and an overall weighted health score, (11) save all inventory and dependency-map JSON files and run cja_component_audit.py to generate a combined HTML report. The report must include: an Executive Summary with combined KPIs across all four types and the overall weighted health score, per-type sections (Segments, Calculated Metrics, Dimensions, Metrics) each with tier breakdowns and duplicate or near-duplicate groups, a Cross-Type Dependencies section showing the segment-to-dimension and calculated-metric-to-metric reference maps with orphaned references highlighted, an Ownership Distribution section covering all four types with single-owner risk flags, and a unified Recommendations section with action items (delete, merge, rename, archive review, tag) attributed to responsible owners.
